summary: An academic OS course project (розрахункова робота) containing Docker Compose–based configurations for deploying WordPress with Nginx (TLS termination) and MySQL. Two near-identical setups exist: wordpress-Khrystyna (created on Windows) and wordpress-struk, each representing a team member's environment.
project_structure: Root contains README.md and two directories: wordpress-Khrystyna/ and wordpress-struk/. Each directory contains a docker-compose.yml defining three services (MySQL db, WordPress FPM, Nginx webserver) and a nginx-conf/ subdirectory with nginx.conf and a self-signed TLS certificate/key pair. The two setups are structurally identical and differ only in naming conventions.
strengths: Consistent and parallel structure across both deployment environments
HTTPS enforced via HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect in Nginx configuration
Credentials externalized through .env file and environment variables
Named Docker volumes for data persistence (wordpress, dbdata)
Custom bridge network for service isolation (app-network)
Lightweight Alpine-based Docker images to minimize image size
Correct use of depends_on for service startup ordering
potential_issues: Private TLS key files (.key) are committed to the public repository — a security risk
No .env file or .env.example provided — required variables are undocumented
README is minimal (one line in Ukrainian) and lacks setup or usage instructions
No .gitignore file present — allowed sensitive files to be tracked
Docker images are significantly outdated (WordPress 5.1.1 from 2019, Nginx 1.15.12 from 2019)
nginx.conf appears to be missing the PHP-FPM fastcgi proxy block, which would prevent PHP from being served
SSL certificate names appear swapped between the two directories (Struk-Khrystyna vs Khrystyna-Struk)
No automated tests or CI/CD pipeline
recommendations: Remove private keys from the repository and git history immediately; add *.key and *.crt to .gitignore
Add a .gitignore file to exclude sensitive files, .env, and editor artifacts
Provide a .env.example with placeholder values for all required environment variables
Expand the README with setup instructions, prerequisites, certificate generation steps, and local hosts file configuration
Upgrade Docker images to current stable versions
Complete the nginx.conf with the missing PHP-FPM fastcgi_pass location block
Consider merging both setups into a single parameterized Compose configuration
